symbols: child among wild animals — the messianic child of Isaiah 11:6 who leads the peaceable kingdom
lion lying with lamb and ox — the eschatological peace where predator and prey coexist
leopard resting calmly — the wild beast tamed in the age of the Messiah
group of pilgrims in background — Penn's treaty or the faithful community entering peace
luminous horizon — the dawning of the messianic age and the new creation
description: A young child in a white dress stands at the center of the composition, holding what appears to be a branch or vine. Surrounding the child are a lion, a leopard, an ox, sheep, and a goat, all resting peacefully together. The lion and ox are side by side at the right. In the background at the left, a group of soberly dressed figures gathers near some trees, possibly engaged in a meeting or treaty. A luminous sky stretches across the horizon between the two groups. The landscape is lush with large trees framing the scene.
theological_description: Edward Hicks painted over sixty versions of the Peaceable Kingdom, each a visual meditation on Isaiah 11:6-9, the great messianic prophecy of cosmic peace. The child who leads the wild animals is the shoot from Jesse's stump (ISA.11.1), the Messiah upon whom the Spirit of the Lord rests. In this age to come, 'the wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the young goat, and the calf and the lion and the fattened calf together; and a little child shall lead them' (ISA.11.6). This vision reverses the curse of Genesis 3, where enmity was placed between creatures. The background group alludes to William Penn's treaty with the Lenape, which Hicks saw as a partial earthly fulfillment of Isaiah's vision. Theologically, the Peaceable Kingdom anticipates the new creation of Revelation 21-22, where death, mourning, and pain are no more, and the knowledge of the Lord fills the earth as waters cover the sea (ISA.11.9).

scripture_refs.reason: The wolf dwelling with the lamb, the child leading the beasts — the direct source of the painting
The shoot from Jesse's stump upon whom the Spirit rests, the messianic ruler who brings this peace
The wolf and lamb feeding together in the new heavens and new earth
The enmity between creatures introduced at the Fall, reversed in the Peaceable Kingdom
The new creation where death and pain are abolished, the eschatological fulfillment of Isaiah's vision
Creation groaning and awaiting liberation — the Peaceable Kingdom as that liberation realized
